rulings.Case Title: YOU XING LIANG VS. LUNG SHAN CHINESE RESTAURANT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set One)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 2, 2014, Line 2, PLAINTIFF YOU XING LIANG Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set One). Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No courtesy copies of the moving papers were provided to the Court. Therefore, the matter is continued to September 11, 2014, at 9:00 am. The Court directs Plaintiff to provide courtesy copies of the moving papers to Dept. 302 pursuant to Local Rule 2.6B by September 2, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SYSCO SAN FRANCISCO, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. TAMRA WIENKE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Pursuant To Ccp 437c / Notice
rulings.Rulings: MATTER ON CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, SEPTEMBER 2, 2014, LINE 6, PLAINTIFF SYSCO SAN FRANCISCO, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Pursuant To Ccp 437c. The motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiff establishes that $9,581.60 is due on the common count claims. See Ableman declaration. Plaintiff shall dismiss the breach of guaranty cause of action by the time of the hearing. The motion for summary judgment does not address that cause of action and plaintiff does not seek summary adjudication in the alternative. Plaintiff is entitled to judgment on the common count claims. No opposition was filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order that complies with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MEHRAN GOLCHEHREH et al VS. SAEED GOLCHEHREH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Plaintiff Mehran Golchehreh And Related Claims Due To Lack Of Standing
rulings.Rulings: MATTER ON CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, SEPTEMBER 2, 2014, LINE 8, DEFENDANT FOOD & LIQUOR WORLD, INC., Motion To Dismiss Plaintiff Mehran Golchehreh And Related Claims Due To Lack Of Standing. The motion to dismiss due to lack of standing is denied without prejudice. The filed motion relies on extrinsic evidence, rather than being based on the face of the complaint. The motion thus cannot be construed as a demurrer or a judgment on the pleadings. If the Court were to treat the motion as a motion for summary judgment, it does not meet the requirements of Code of Civil Procedure section 437c. (See 5 Witkin, Cal. Procedure (5th ed. 2008) Pleading, §§ 1004-1006, pp. 412-418; Pianka v. California (1956) Cal. 2d 208.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JASON LUCERO VS. MARIA ANGELIKA FREITAG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents Nos. 1-6, 8, 10-17, 19-21, & 23-29 And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, September 2, 2014, Line 4, DEFENDANT MARIA FREITAG Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents Nos. 1-6, 8, 10-17, 19-21, & 23-29 And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted in part and denied in part. As to Requests 1-4, 8, 12, 16, 20 and 24-29, the motion is denied. As to Request Nos. 5, 6, 13-15, 17, 19 and 21, the motion is granted and Plaintiff shall produce all responsive documents in his possession, custody or control (short of requesting or subpoenaing records from other entities) or state whether he has already done so or state that he is unable to do so in compliance with CCP 2031.210(a). This would include medical records and pay stubs. As to Request No. 23, the motion is granted, Plaintiff shall produce any independently prepared witness statements or state whether any exist in compliance with CCP 2031.210(a). All requests for sanctions as to this motion are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PROLOGIS INC, A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. MONICA TAYLOR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: MATTER ON CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, SEPTEMBER 2, 2014, LINE 12, PLAINTIFF PROLOGIS INC, A DELAWARE CORPORATION Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction. Denied. Plaintiff has not established that the Court has the authority to order the requested preliminary injunction. In Pena v. Toney (1979) 98 Cal.App.3d 534, 539, the case cited by Plaintiff as precedent for granting such relief, the court declined to comment on the propriety of the preliminary injunction. Even if the Court had authority, the Court cannot determine based on the evidence provided and with the amount of certainty required for the drastic remedy of preventing Defendant's attorneys from being paid until this case is reduced to judgment, that Defendant is paying her attorneys with embezzled funds. Plaintiff's request for judicial notice is granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)
